name: afflib-tools
title: afflib-tools
description:
Advanced Forensics Format Library (utilities)
The Advanced Forensic Format (AFF) is on-disk format for storing
computer forensic information. Critical features of AFF include:
.
- AFF allows you to store both computer forensic data and associated
metadata in one or more files.
- AFF allows files to be digital signed, to provide for
chain-of-custody and long-term file integrity.
- AFF allows for forensic disk images to stored encrypted and
decrypted on-the-fly for processing. This allows disk images
containing privacy sensitive material to be stored on the Internet.
.
This package provides the AFF Toolkit, that is a set of programs for
working with computer forensic information. Using these tools you can:
* Interconvert disk images between a variety of formats, including:
- raw or "dd";
- splitraw (in which a single image is split between multiple files);
- AFF format (in which the entire disk image is stored in a single
file);
- AFD format (in which a disk image is stored in multiple AFF files
stored in a single directory);
- AFM format (in which an AFF file is used to annotate a raw file).
* Compare disk images and report the data or metadata that is different.
* Copy disk images from one location to another, with full verification
of data, metadata, and the automatic generation of a chain-of-custody
segment.
* Find errors in an AFF file and fix them.
* Print information about a file.
* Print detailed statistics about a file
* Generate a XML representation of a disk image's metadata (for example,
acquisition time or the serial number of the acquisition device).
* Produce a XML "diskprint" which allows a disk image to be rapidly
fingerprinted without having the computer the SHA1 of the entire
disk.
.
The AFF Toolkit provides these executables: affcat, affcompare, affconvert,
affcopy, affcrypto, affdiskprint, affinfo, affix, affrecover, affsegment,
affsign, affstats, affuse, affverify and affxml.

commands.name: affcat
commands.description: Output contents of an image file to stdout.

arguments.description: Output BADFALG for bad blocks (default is to output NULLs).
arguments.raw: -b  Output BADFALG for bad blocks (default is to output NULLs).

arguments.description: Debug mode. Print the page numbers to stderr as data goes to stdout.
arguments.raw: -d  Debug mode. Print the page numbers to stderr as data goes to stdout.

arguments.description: List Long. Prints segment names, lengths, and args.
arguments.raw: -L  List Long. Prints segment names, lengths, and args.

arguments.description: List all the segment names, rather than outputting data.
arguments.raw: -l  List all the segment names, rather than outputting data.

arguments.description: Noisy mode. Tell when pages are skipped.
arguments.raw: -n  Noisy mode. Tell when pages are skipped.

arguments.description: Quiet mode (default). Don't print to STDERR if a page is skipped because it is not
arguments.raw: -q  Quiet mode (default). Don't print to STDERR if a page is skipped because it is not

arguments.description: Just print the version number and exit.
arguments.raw: -v  Just print the version number and exit.

commands.name: affcompare
commands.description: compare the contents of an ISO file to an AFF file

commands.name: affconvert
commands.description: convert files between RAW and AFF formats

commands.name: affinfo
commands.description: print information about an AFF file

commands.name: affrecover
commands.description: recover broken pages of an AFF file

commands.name: affstats
commands.description: print specific statistics about one or more AFF files

commands.name: affverify
commands.description: verify the digital signature on a signed file

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use affcat because Advanced Forensics Format Library (utilities).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use affcat as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `affcat [options] image [images]` as documented upstream for affcat.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair affcat with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `affcat --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install afflib. Advanced Forensics Format Library (utilities).
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Advanced Forensics Format Library (utilities).
